response.justification: While Bogdan is an exceptional software engineer with 19+ years of experience and strong architectural and mentoring skills, he is fundamentally misaligned with this role's core requirement. His C++ experience is minimal (6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015) and a decade-old; his entire career trajectory since then has shifted toward JavaScript/TypeScript, DLang, and web infrastructure. The role explicitly demands mid-senior C++ expertise with multi-threading, networking, and industrial protocols—domains where the candidate has no recent or demonstrated experience. His education (BS Computer Science, BA Fine Arts) also differs from the stated B.E./B.Tech/M.E./M.Tech requirement. He would require substantial ramp-up time in both the language and the industrial automation domain.
strengths: Strong track record of distributed-systems architecture and event-driven platform design
Excellent mentoring and knowledge-sharing capabilities, demonstrated through workshops and open-source contributions
Deep software craftsmanship discipline with proven mastery of TDD, CI/CD, and agile methodologies
concerns: C++ expertise is minimal and severely outdated—only 6 months of professional experience in 2014–2015, with no recent hands-on work
Career trajectory has diverged from systems programming into modern web stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React), indicating deprioritization of low-level language skills
No demonstrated experience with industrial automation, IoT protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), secure development standards (IEC 62443), or the manufacturing domain
